feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to estimate SUM directory disk size requirements for SAP Release Upgrades or S4 Conversion
entry.content:
Dear Guru's,

We have a requirement to perform a S4 Conversion upgrade from EHP8 to S4Hana 2023.

We need to know the disk space needed for SUM folder for the Upgrade.

SAP System Source Details:
On-Prem
Database: HANA
OS:SLES
Database size:500 GB

Target:
S4Hana 2023.

No DMO needed only S4 Conversion.

Do we have any thumb rule for calculating the SUM folder disk space requirements ?

I could see from documents that space of 50 GB is needed but could not see any specific thumb rule or any calculations for SUM folder. 

How we can calculate SUM folder space requirement before hand? I also checked Sizing guide could not find any

Kindly do the needful

Thanks in advance

Regards,
Mithran

entry.title: Logging table SAPWSD./1CADMC/00000262 in source system is very large
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Logging table SAPWSD./1CADMC/00000262 in source system  is very large, attached is the screenshot for your reference. 

So we were checking as this is not an application table and is a SLT related Logging table So what's the best practice to reduce its growth ? is it table paritioning ? or is it reloading the table again ?

We have gone through some notes and found out that SAP suggests Hash partioning as per note 2014562

Please confirm how we proceed to fix this ?

entry.title: F0366 - My spend new app - Need to change period from Month-to-date to Year-to-date
entry.content:
Hi SAP community! 👋

I'm currently working with the Fiori app F0366 – My Spend (new version) and I'm trying to figure out if it's still possible to switch the period view from Month-to-Date to Year-to-Date.

📌 Maybe I'm wrong, but seems this option was available in the older version of the app, but I can't seem to find it in the new one.

A few questions I'd love to get input on:

  • Has this option been removed or relocated in the new version?
  • Is there a configuration or personalization setting I might be missing?
  • Could this be related to a role/authorization restriction?
  • Any workaround or alternative app that provides a Year-to-Date spend view?

Would appreciate any feedback from those who have worked on this app, whether from a functional, Basis, or Fiori/UI5 perspective. 🙏

S/4 HANA On-premise 2023 SP02

entry.title: How to rotate text in smartforms to 90 degrees?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I've requirement in smartforms, to print the text in 90 degrees vertically. I've tried the below method. But now working.

Smartform :

 

In SPAD T-code, I've created 2 print controls to the respective device type with below value and I've used them in smartforms also but text is printed as a normal text.

Output:

Expected:

 

how to fix this?

I don't know how much degrees to rotate. Kindly anyone suggest the solution.

 

Thanks,

Naveen.

entry.title: regarding enhancement in RFFOEDI1 program to generate IDocs EDI 820 address field
entry.content: We are using RFFOEDI1 program to generate IDocs EDI 820, we received one requirement from User that, while generating the IDocs in Address segment 13, Building Number required in new line instead of same street number line.

entry.title: SAP HCM to Third Party Integration (LIve)
entry.content: SAP HCM master data needs to be updated in our third-party systems.  How can this be achieved in real time? We are not able to find any time stamp or event triggered at the time of data change/update in infotypes? What is the SAP recommended approach for real-time HR master data update propagation to external systems? We are unable to find an SAP note.

entry.title: Do ERP Systems Really Need Machine Learning? Exploring SAP HANA PAL in SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems have traditionally been designed to manage and process business transactions efficiently. Their primary role has been to capture, store, and organize business data across functions such as Sales, Procurement, Finance, Manufacturing, and Supply Chain.

However, modern organizations expect more from their ERP systems. Businesses are no longer satisfied with simply storing historical data; they want systems that can analyze information, identify patterns, predict future outcomes, and support decision-making.

This raises an important question:

Do ERP systems really need Machine Learning?

The answer depends on the business problem being addressed. Machine Learning should not be implemented merely because the technology is available. Instead, it should be used where it can provide measurable business value.

As part of my research on SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) Procedures in SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), I explored how built-in Machine Learning algorithms can be applied directly within the SAP ecosystem to solve real business challenges.

This blog summarizes some of the key Machine Learning capabilities available through SAP HANA PAL and their relevance in ERP scenarios.

What is SAP HANA PAL?

The Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) is a collection of Machine Learning and statistical algorithms that run directly inside the SAP HANA database.

Rather than exporting data to external Machine Learning platforms, PAL enables organizations to perform predictive analytics where the data already resides.

Some key advantages include:

  • Reduced data movement

  • Improved performance

  • Better data governance

  • Integration with SAP applications and business processes

  • Real-time analytics capabilities

PAL provides algorithms for:

  • Time Series Forecasting

  • Classification

  • Regression

  • Clustering

  • Association Rules

  • Outlier Detection

  • Recommendation Scenarios

Let's explore how these capabilities can be used in ERP environments.

1. Time Series Forecasting

Forecasting is one of the most valuable Machine Learning applications in ERP systems.

Organizations continuously need to estimate future demand, sales, inventory levels, and procurement requirements.

Using historical business data, forecasting algorithms can predict future trends and support proactive planning.

Typical ERP Use Cases

Sales Forecasting

Predict future sales based on historical transactions and seasonal patterns.

Production Planning

Estimate manufacturing demand and optimize production schedules.

Inventory Planning

Maintain optimal stock levels while reducing overstocking and stock shortages.

Procurement Planning

Predict material requirements and improve purchasing decisions.

Business Value

  • Improved demand planning

  • Reduced inventory carrying costs

  • Better resource utilization

  • Enhanced supply chain efficiency

2. Classification

Classification algorithms predict predefined categories or outcomes based on historical data.

In ERP systems, classification can help organizations estimate probabilities and support decision-making processes.

Example: Quotation Conversion Prediction

Many companies generate thousands of sales quotations every year. However, only a portion of these quotations eventually become sales orders.

Using classification models, organizations can analyze historical quotation data and predict:

  • Which quotations are likely to convert

  • Which opportunities require additional attention

  • Which customers have higher purchase probability

Additional ERP Use Cases

Customer Churn Prediction

Identify customers who are likely to stop doing business with the company.

Supplier Risk Classification

Classify suppliers based on delivery performance, quality metrics, and compliance factors.

Payment Default Prediction

Identify customers with a higher likelihood of delayed or missed payments.

Business Value

  • Improved sales effectiveness

  • Better risk management

  • Enhanced customer retention

  • Stronger supplier governance

3. Regression

While classification predicts categories, regression predicts continuous numerical values.

Regression algorithms are highly useful when organizations need estimates for future business metrics.

ERP Use Cases

Revenue Prediction

Forecast future revenue based on historical trends and business drivers.

Sales Value Forecasting

Estimate the expected value of upcoming sales opportunities.

Procurement Cost Estimation

Predict material procurement costs using historical pricing data.

Product Price Forecasting

Estimate future product pricing trends.

Customer Lifetime Value Analysis

Predict the long-term value of customer relationships.

Business Value

  • Improved budgeting and planning

  • Better financial forecasting

  • Data-driven pricing strategies

  • Enhanced profitability analysis

4. Association Rules

Association Rule Mining identifies relationships between products, services, and customer transactions.

This technique is widely used in retail, e-commerce, and sales analytics.

Example

Suppose customers frequently purchase:

  • Product A

  • Product B

together within the same transaction.

Association Rule algorithms can discover this pattern and provide actionable recommendations.

ERP Use Cases

Product Recommendations

Suggest related products during sales processes.

Cross-Selling Opportunities

Identify complementary products that increase sales revenue.

Product Bundling

Create optimized product packages based on purchasing behavior.

Customer Buying Pattern Analysis

Understand customer purchasing habits and preferences.

Business Value

  • Increased sales revenue

  • Improved customer experience

  • Better marketing effectiveness

  • Stronger product strategy

5. Clustering

Clustering is an unsupervised Machine Learning technique used to group similar records without predefined labels.

It helps organizations uncover hidden patterns within business data.

ERP Use Cases

Customer Segmentation

Group customers based on purchasing behavior, revenue, geography, or demographics.

Supplier Segmentation

Categorize suppliers according to performance, risk, or strategic importance.

Product Segmentation

Identify product groups with similar sales or profitability characteristics.

Regional Demand Analysis

Analyze demand patterns across different regions.

Business Value

  • Better customer targeting

  • Improved supplier management

  • More effective marketing campaigns

  • Enhanced strategic planning

Do ERP Systems Really Need Machine Learning?

The answer is:

Not every ERP process requires Machine Learning.

Core ERP functionality such as transaction processing, accounting, order management, and inventory tracking can continue operating effectively without predictive models.

However, when organizations seek answers to questions such as:

  • What will happen next?

  • Which customers are likely to buy?

  • Which suppliers pose risks?

  • How much inventory will be needed?

  • What revenue can be expected?

Machine Learning becomes highly valuable.

ERP systems contain vast amounts of historical business data. Machine Learning enables organizations to transform that data into actionable insights and intelligent decision support.

Key Takeaway

Machine Learning should not be implemented simply because it is available.

The real objective should always be:

"Which business problem are we trying to solve?"

When applied to the right use cases, Machine Learning can transform ERP systems from transaction-processing platforms into intelligent business platforms.

One of the most exciting aspects of SAP HANA PAL is that these capabilities can be executed directly within the SAP ecosystem, reducing the need for external Machine Learning platforms while keeping analytics close to the data.

As I continue exploring SAP HANA PAL Procedures in SAP BTP, it is becoming increasingly clear that Forecasting, Classification, Regression, Association Rules, and Clustering have significant potential to bring intelligence into ERP processes and help organizations make smarter decisions.

References

  • SAP HANA Predictive Analysis Library (PAL)

  • SAP HANA Machine Learning Capabilities

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP)

  • SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analytics

entry.title: Standard inventory ageing report in S/4 HANA
entry.content: Hi, I am looking for a standard inventory ageing report that can be run within S4. Can anyone help me with this? I've seen that discussion on the Inventory Aging report in SAP Fiori, but I need to know more about it if it exists. Thanks a lot.

entry.title: RSP Agent failing to connect to SLD Server - "Failed to connect to SLD server" error
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am trying to configure the SAP Business One Remote Support Platform (RSP) agent, but I am repeatedly getting a "Failed to connect to SLD server" pop-up error when clicking Test Connection.

Current Status & Troubleshooting Conducted:Services: I have confirmed that both the SAP Business One Client Agent and SAP Business One RSP Agent Service are actively Running and set to Automatic in Windows Services.Port Availability: Tested connection capability via port 40000.

Despite these steps, the RSP agent still refuses to handshake with the System Landscape Directory.

Could this be related to a strict SSL certificate rejection by the RSP agent for self-signed certificates, or is there a specific database mapping error I might be missing on the SLD side? Any advice on how to bypass this or check deeper error logs would be highly appreciated.

entry.title: HANA Studio is not showing calculation views flow diagrams correctly.
entry.content: Hi SAP,HANA Studio is not showing calculation views flow diagrams correctly. We started facing the issues with recent HANA Studio update to 2.3.88

entry.title: identify customer posted items SAP HANA S4
entry.content: How to identify my customer posted items which are not cleared yet using SAP HANA S4?

entry.title: Created By User Changed After /SAPAPO/CCR Transfer from APO to ECC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We observed a scenario in our APO–ECC integration landscape where, after executing a repair/transfer activity in transaction /SAPAPO/CCR, the “Created By” user of the process/planned order in ECC (COOISPI) is showing as the user who executed the CCR transfer.

Scenario:

  1. As part of clearing CIF inconsistencies plant-wise through /SAPAPO/CCR, users execute transfer/repair activities without identifying any specific order individually.
  2. After the inconsistencies are corrected and orders are recreated/transferred in ECC, the “Created By” field in COOISPI shows the user who executed the CCR activity.

Questions:

  • Is this standard SAP behavior?
  • Does CCR technically recreate the order using the logged-in user ID?
  • Is it possible to retain the original creator or RFC user instead of the CCR execution user?
  • We also observed that this behavior is happening only for Sales Order related planned/process orders which are recreated or converted by the CCR execution user. Other SFG level material orders are not showing this behavior. Is there any specific reason for this difference?

Environment:
APO PPDS integrated with ECC Process Orders.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: I need to handle vendor batch traceability and separate batches per arrival in SAP S/4HANA.
entry.content: Our main concern is maintaining traceability of vendor batches, creating a separate batch for each vendor batch respecting the expiration date. We want to record stock in transit and its value upon shipping from the origin, and also record the invoice before goods arrive, for better planning and payments. Previously, with movement 107 in older SAP versions, we could generate stock in transit without creating a batch, and then create batches upon arrival with movement 109 that match vendor batches and expiration dates. In SAP S/4HANA, movement 107 generates a batch that we can't modify upon arrival, causing all imported goods to be recorded in a single batch instead of multiple vendor batches. How can we follow the same process to respect each vendor batch and expiration date in SAP S/4HANA?

entry.title: the memory drop down box appears but you are no longer able to click on it and use it.
entry.content: In multiple transactions, when the history / memory drop down appears when typing in a field, your history is appearing but you cannot select it. You click on it and it does nothing. We use to be able to click on it and it would take it into the box.

entry.title: DBCOを使用したOracle への接続について
entry.content:
お世話になっております。

 DBCOを使用したOracle への接続について確認させてください。

 【対象】 S/4HANA 2022 / 2023 / 2025

 【質問】
上記対象からDBCO接続可能なOracleバージョンを確認したいです。

・技術的に接続可能な構成は存在するか
・SAPサポート対象か
・サポート対象外の場合、制限事項は何か 

参考となる資料などありましたらご教授いただけますでしょうか。

 

以上、よろしくお願いいたします。

entry.title: Hana DB Audit Policy
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We would like to know about the HANA DB audit policy as we want to create policy in our system. Could you please help us to set up the policy as per the guidelines.

Please let us know if there are not SAP Notes/ blogs which we need to follow.


Regards
Mahendra

entry.title: Table names for Process order in sap hana
entry.content:
Hi All,
Currently my team is working on the process order data in sap hana and we are looking certain fields into sap hana.
Thanks for the help .

Raw Target Quantity
Raw Actual Quantity
Actual Run Time
Target Run Time
Input Product
Total Actual Quantity
Total Target Quantity

entry.title: While doing MIRO business got the error negative vaule
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Issue: While posting the MIRO business got the error “moving average price for material be negative” since they have parked to document but while posting got the error below is screenshot.

Business wanted to post the MIRO document, as already posted

entry.title: Using SSO with Hana 2.0 and Azure AD
entry.content:
We have SAP Hana DB 2.00.079 and i want to login XS Classic engine with Azure AD. I created a Azure test account, configure single sign-on with SAML and everything working as expected.

I changed Hana side config/certificates for Azure prod but I couldn't login with prod Azure account. I enabled the traces and I can see below error.
Authentication SAMLAuthenticator.cpp(01122) : %%% [error: 4040007] [correlation id: 0x9e9a3ab65af736fe] Internal error occurred: Invalid principal id for principal .
XSSession XSSessionLifecycle.cpp(00379) : Assertion authentication for user xxx@xxx.com failed with reason: ptime::PtimeException

I am login Azure test account with user/pass. Prod Azure account working with windows session, so it is using X509 login to Azure.

I checked both Prod and Test Azure SAML Responses, key differences like below.

ProdTest
<Subject>
<NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress">xxx@xxx.com</NameID>
<Subject>
<NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified">xxx@xxx.com</NameID>

<AttributeStatement>

<Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnmethodsreferences">
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/x509</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/multipleauthn</AttributeValue>
</Attribute>

<AttributeStatement>

<Attribute Name="http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnmethodsreferences">
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/password</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/multipleauthn</AttributeValue>
<AttributeValue>http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/authenticationmethod/unspecified</AttributeValue>
</Attribute>
<AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2026-06-23T06:58:06.566Z" SessionIndex="_ef3f88b7-4876-40c6-b948-0c838ab31c00">
<AuthnContext>
<AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:Unspecified</AuthnContextClassRef>
</AuthnContext>
</AuthnStatement>
<AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2026-06-19T08:47:16.019Z" SessionIndex="_b98a9868-70a8-4137-a071-eef985d76100">
<AuthnContext>
<AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:Password</AuthnContextClassRef>
</AuthnContext>
</AuthnStatement>

Hana can read to user NameID from SAML Response but i couldn't match the user. I hope someone can share some clue.

entry.title: RedHat Compatible version for S4HANA 2025 upgrade
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We are planning for S4 HANA upgrade to 2025 from 2022.
Current OS = RHEL 8.10 for both SAP application and DB server.
Current HANA DB = 2.00.79.04

Question:- 
1. We want to upgrade OS to RHEL 9.6. Is RHEL 9.7 supported by SAP for S4 HANA upgrade 2025?
2. If we stick to RHEL 9.6, then what happens to minor OS patching and how to handle monthly bug fixes from RedHat?
3. Do we have to freeze minor patching of RHEL 9.6 until RHEL 9.7 is certified by SAP?

We have referred SAP note 2235581.

Please let us know.

Thanks, Ramesh
